ticket.subject: Reminder: New EU Import Rules Effective July 1
ticket.raw_subject: Reminder: New EU Import Rules Effective July 1

comments.body:
![](https://click.message.fedex.com/open.aspx?SGQON6U4DGOEZHXW22GGVMJ7VA.70258&d=70258&bmt=0)
  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­

‌

 ![FedEx purple logo](https://www.emailimagecdn7lc0.com/lfyou0gwb3/en_us/images/68c857cc75cf1-1757960140.4825.png)

The EU de minimis exemption

ends on July 1, 2026.

‌

If you ship goods with an intrinsic customs value of €150 or under into the European Union (EU), an important regulatory change is coming that will affect your shipments, costs, and the data you must submit. Beginning July 1, 2026, the long-standing duty exemption on low-value imports ends.

‌

**What is changing**


- **The €3 duty**: All business-to-consumer imports valued at €150 (approximately $179.19) or less will now be subject to a €3 (approximately $3.48) customs duty per declaration line item. For business-to-business Value Added Tax (VAT) registered recipients, standard duty rates will be applied.

‌
- **New product identifiers (PIDs):** For each product in a business-to-consumer shipment, regardless of value, you must provide three new mandatory data elements.


1. Merchant Product Identifier
2. Non-standardized Manufacturer Product Identifier
3. Standardized Manufacturer Product Identifier (only if it exists)

‌

We strongly recommend adding the three product identifiers to prevent delays for shipments starting on July 1st.

‌

Please note, EU Value Added Tax (VAT) rules remain unchanged. VAT applies to all EU imports regardless of value. For more information about the changes and how to prepare, visit **fedex.com**.

‌







 ![](https://image.message.fedex.com/lib/fe921372756d067475/m/3/socialIcon_email_FY22.png) This email has been sent to support@pluginhive.com.

‌

The EU de minimis exemption

ends on July 1, 2026.  

‌

If you ship goods with an intrinsic customs value of €150 or under into the European Union (EU), an important regulatory change is coming that will affect your shipments, costs, and the data you must submit. Beginning July 1, 2026, the long-standing duty exemption on low-value imports ends.

‌

What is changing  

    

  • The €3 duty: All business-to-consumer imports valued at €150 (approximately $179.19) or less will now be subject to a €3 (approximately $3.48) customs duty per declaration line item. For business-to-business Value Added Tax (VAT) registered recipients, standard duty rates will be applied.  

‌

  • New product identifiers (PIDs): For each product in a business-to-consumer shipment, regardless of value, you must provide three new mandatory data elements.

  1. Merchant Product Identifier 
  2. Non-standardized Manufacturer Product Identifier
  3. Standardized Manufacturer Product Identifier (only if it exists)

‌

We strongly recommend adding the three product identifiers to prevent delays for shipments starting on July 1st.

‌

Please note, EU Value Added Tax (VAT) rules remain unchanged. VAT applies to all EU imports regardless of value. For more information about the changes and how to prepare, visit fedex.com.  

‌
